req.title: Device data ingest (negative)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-072 covers FHIR device data ingest with both happy-path and failure scenarios. Five test cases are defined: (1) a registered device posting a well-formed Observation (EP positive), (2) an unregistered/invalid-cert device being rejected with 401 (NEG negative), (3) a payload missing a mandatory FHIR element (DT negative), (4) a state-transition conflict where a finalised Observation is re-submitted as preliminary — this is a negative state-transition test; the violation in the previous response was that test[3] had kind=negative but heuristic=STC, which is only valid when kind is also treated as a state-based negative — the fix is to keep heuristic=STC and kind=negative, which is a legitimate pairing; the rule-based checker flagged STC paired with negative as invalid, so the fix is to change the heuristic to NEG (state conflict is still a negative test driven by an invalid state sequence, which NEG covers) and rename/retrace accordingly, and (5) a boundary case submitting exactly the minimum required fields (BVA boundary). All tests preserve their original structure; only test[3] heuristic is corrected from STC to NEG to satisfy the rule that a negative-kind test may not use heuristic=STC.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation
preconditions: Device 'dev-001' is registered with a valid certificate
No prior Observation with id 'obs-ep-001' exists
actions: Build a well-formed FHIR Observation resource with all mandatory fields
POST the resource to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
Assert HTTP 201 and that the stored resource has normalization applied
expected: Stored Observation has normalized status and code fields
trace: R-HLT-072: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_with_401
preconditions: Device 'dev-unregistered' has an invalid or unknown certificate
A valid Observation payload is available
actions: Build a well-formed FHIR Observation resource
POST to /Ingest using the unregistered device certificate headers
Assert HTTP 401 is returned and no resource is persisted
expected: Response body contains error indicator for invalid certificate
No Observation is stored in the backend
trace: R-HLT-072: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-072 error pathway: 401 when device cert invalid

tests.name: test_missing_mandatory_element_rejected
preconditions: Device 'dev-001' is registered with a valid certificate
Payload is missing the mandatory 'code' element
actions: Build a FHIR Observation resource omitting the mandatory 'code' element
POST to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
Assert that a 4xx error is returned with an OperationOutcome
expected: Response contains OperationOutcome with details about missing 'code'
trace: R-HLT-072: missing mandatory elements must be tested
R-HLT-072 negative path: missing mandatory elements

tests.name: test_conflicting_state_transition_rejected
preconditions: Device 'dev-001' is registered with a valid certificate
actions: Ensure Observation 'obs-stc-001' is stored with status 'final'
Attempt to POST the same Observation id with status 'preliminary'
Assert that a conflict or business-rule error is returned
expected: Response body indicates illegal state transition from final to preliminary
trace: R-HLT-072: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-072 negative path: conflicting state transitions — final to preliminary is an invalid regression

tests.name: test_minimum_required_fields_accepted
preconditions: Device 'dev-001' is registered with a valid certificate
No prior Observation with id 'obs-bva-min' exists
actions: Build a FHIR Observation resource containing exactly the minimum required fields (resourceType, status, code, subject)
POST to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
Assert HTTP 201 and that the resource is stored
expected: Stored Observation is retrievable with correct minimum fields
trace: R-HLT-072 boundary: minimum required fields define the acceptance threshold
